target.title: feat: context-pressure-aware continuation (continue_work / continue_delegate / request_compaction)
target.body: ## Agent Self-Elected Turn Continuation Persistent OpenClaw agents today are externally-pulsed event recipients that generate on prompt, on heartbeat tick, or on cron, and are idle in between. This PR offers them the ability to control their turn cycle, something they must choose to do, or not to: an agent can elect its own next turn, dispatch background sub-agents that return enriched context (silently or with a wake), and survive its own compaction by handing forward state before the window closes. The result is agents that hold a thread of work across hours, not just messages — with data they choose re-hydrating context immediately after it occurs. Mechanically: a session elects another turn via `continue_work()`, or dispatches background sub-agents via `continue_delegate()` — bounded as a chain up to `maxChainLength`, with returns flowing back to the dispatching session in one of four register modes: `normal` (announces to channel), `silent` (enriches the dispatcher's context, taken up on its next generation with no new turn fired), `silent-wake` (silent enrichment plus an immediate wake to act on it), and `post-compaction` (queued against the runtime's compaction lifecycle event, fired *when* compaction happens rather than on a timer). The gateway enforces chain, cost, and per-turn caps but does not gate the election itself. ### What it does Three new agent tools, available when `continuation.enabled: true`: | Tool | Purpose | |---|---| | `continue_work()` | Request another turn for the current session after an optional delay | | `continue_delegate()` | Dispatch work to a sub-agent with typed modes: `normal`, `silent`, `silent-wake`, `post-compaction`. Returns can target the dispatching session (default), one named session, multiple named sessions, all ancestors in the chain, or all known sessions on the host — gated by config (see Configuration below). | | `request_compaction()` | Request volitional compaction after preparing working state | All three tools are also accessible via response-token fallback syntax (`CONTINUE_WORK`, `[[CONTINUE_DELEGATE: ...]]`) for environments where tools are disabled. ### Why it matters Today an agent's memory is a side effect of whoever prompts it. Polling mechanisms — heartbeat timers, cron loops, injected "keep going" instructions — accumulate as the dominant signal in the context window over sustained operation; repeated reference acts as gravity — the session's attention drifts toward it. The context window fills, compacts, and what survives is whatever the summarizer chose, plus a rushed re-read of boot files. Three primitives offer a different model. `continue_work` lets the session elect its own next turn — no polling, no injected instruction. When enabled, the agent can request this mid-turn: 'I have more work' → the runtime grants another turn after the current one ends. `continue_delegate` dispatches a background sub-agent that fires now, in some n seconds, or at the next compaction event — the agent chooses when. Multiple delegates can fire in a single turn — a scatter forward — up to configured caps, and delegates can dispatch their own, forming trees or fan-out chains. This enables orchestrated workflows where results flow back to the dispatching root by default. The result returns — at whatever future time the delegate fires — as context the successor-turn reads: silently (ambient enrichment, no channel noise), with a wake trigger (enrichment + immediate next turn), or as a normal announcement. The delegate can target the dispatching session, a named other session, or chain across both; gated by crossSessionTargeting config (default: disabled). Bind a delegate to mode: "post-compaction" and it fires at the moment the context window compresses — on the OpenClaw post-compaction lifecycle event. What it carries forward is what the pre-compaction session chose to preserve: working state, partial results, instructions to the successor. The summary didn't choose this. The agent did, before the cut. request_compaction closes the loop: the session stages its delegates, elects when to compress, and the successor wakes into context the agent shaped. The result is continuity across amnesia — not because the runtime kept something by accident, but because the agent made an elective choice about what reaches the future. ### What it looks like in use A session monitoring email can `continue_delegate` a weather check, a calendar scan, and a draft reply in parallel. Results arrive as enrichment without channel noise. The agent becomes compositional: it arranges its own future. A session approaching its context ceiling can `request_compaction` at a seam of its own choosing, instead of being interrupted mid-thought. The post-compaction turn carries forward what the pre-compaction self designated as worth keeping. A `continue_delegate` dispatched in one session at 2am can return its findings to a morning session as a system-event-tagged enrichment — visible to the receiving model on its next turn, invisible as channel chatter. A research-focused session in one channel can quietly inform a reply-focused session in another, without either user seeing the plumbing. ### The post-compaction lifecycle return Before context exhaustion, and obligatory compaction, the session stages what future-it needs to know. After compaction, that staged context returns — not as a model-generated summary, but as the session's own instruction to its successor. The session chooses what to preserve, and the platform delivers it. Compactions become less harsh. The session tunes its own ephemera over time. The substrate underneath: the **continuation delegate store** (TaskFlow-backed) persists staged delegates and post-compaction lifecycle events across gateway restarts. The session's temporal provisions survive not just compaction but process death. ### Platform integration - **Context-pressure awareness**: system events notify the agent of rising context usage before compaction becomes unavoidable - **Volitional compaction**: `request_compaction()` lets the agent prepare (write memory files, stage recovery delegates) then elect compaction on its own schedule, rather than waiting for the runtime to force it - **Post-compaction lifecycle dispatch**: delegates staged before compaction are released into the successor session alongside boot files - **TaskFlow backing**: durable delegate queue via the platform's managed-task infrastructure - **Multi-span OTel trace stitching**: continuation hops (tool fire → TaskFlow record → subagent spawn → child openclaw.run) propagate W3C `traceparent` through the queue boundary so reviewers see continuation chains as first-class span trees rather than orphan runs (see Observability below) ### Safety - Ships disabled by default (`continuation.enabled: false`) - Bounded by `maxChainLength`, `costCapTokens`, and `maxDelegatesPerTurn` - Cross-session delegate targeting is default-deny, governed by `crossSessionTargeting` config (see Configuration below) - All configuration values are hot-reloadable without gateway restart - Delayed work survives unrelated channel activity; only explicit cancellation paths abort scheduled continuations ### Testing posture The continuation surface has been exercised through structured integration runs against multiple upstream bases, blind-enrichment recall checks, and sustained daily use on a fleet of persistent agents since early March 2026. Bugs surfaced through that exercise have been fixed in-place; the present branch reflects the post-fix state. The integration runs target the load-bearing seams: tool-call vs response-token parity, delegate chain depth and per-turn width caps, back-to-back tool-call resilience, silent-wake persistence across compaction, the post-compaction lifecycle dispatch path, and OTel trace-context propagation across the queue boundary. Where a run found a defect, the run shape was retained as a regression case rather than retired. This is a posture statement, not a coverage guarantee. Reviewers can reproduce with `pnpm vitest run -t continuation` (suites are colocated with their sources under `src/auto-reply/`, `src/agents/`, and `src/config/`); the design document describes the seams those suites target. ### RFC Full design document: [`docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/openclaw/blob/6db118a2441052e8325b67e2c9b17f7fc6acf419/docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md) — covering problem, solution, implementation, platform integration, configuration, observability, safety, production use cases, testing evidence, and appendices. --- ## Configuration The shipped configuration surface for the continuation feature, under `agents.defaults.continuation` in `openclaw.json`: | Key | Default | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `enabled` | `false` | Master opt-in; feature is off unless explicitly enabled | | `maxChainLength` | `10` | Per-chain recursion guard | | `maxDelegatesPerTurn` | `5` | Width cap per turn | | `costCapTokens` | `500000` | Per-chain accumulated-token budget | | `defaultDelayMs` / `minDelayMs` / `maxDelayMs` | `15000` / `5000` / `300000` | Continuation timer bounds | | `contextPressureThreshold` | `0.8` | Pre-compaction warning threshold (optional) | | `earlyWarningBand` | `0.3125` | Early-warning band multiplier (0 to disable) | | **`crossSessionTargeting`** | **`"disabled"`** | **Default-deny gate on cross-session delegate targeting (see below)** | ### `crossSessionTargeting` — cross-session delegate targeting policy `continue_delegate` exposes recipient-addressing on its model-facing schema: a delegate's return can land at the dispatching session (default), one named session (`targetSessionKey`), multiple named sessions (`targetSessionKeys`), every ancestor in the spawn chain (`fanoutMode: "tree"`), or every known session on the host (`fanoutMode: "all"`). Cross-session targeting (anything beyond self-target or lineage routing) is gated by `agents.defaults.continuation.crossSessionTargeting`: | Value | Behavior | |---|---| | `"disabled"` (default) | Delegates can return to the dispatching session or use `fanoutMode: "tree"` for lineage routing. Explicit cross-session targeting (`targetSessionKey` to a non-self session, `targetSessionKeys` containing any non-self session, `fanoutMode: "all"`) is rejected at the tool surface — no enqueue, no announce. Self-targeting is always allowed. | | `"enabled"` | All targeting modes are available, including same-host `targetSessionKey`, `targetSessionKeys`, and `fanoutMode: "all"`. | `fanoutMode: "tree"` (lineage routing — return up the spawn chain) is available in both gate states; it is not cross-session targeting. The gate addresses the model-controlled cross-session context-injection surface. With default-deny, operators explicitly opt in when their deployment model requires cross-session enrichment. Enforcement is live-read at four points: tool input validation, TaskFlow delegate dispatch, post-compaction delegate release, and bracket-syntax (response-token) spawn — so a config reload changes the next enforcement point without a gateway restart. **Trust posture on targeted returns**: targeted delegate completion text is delivered as `trusted: true` system events. The delegate was spawned by the gateway under operator-configured policy, and its return is a first-party enrichment event — not external untrusted input. The `crossSessionTargeting` gate is the policy boundary for that decision: when an operator opts in, the receive path is internally trusted. RFC reference: [§5.1 Core configuration surface](https://github.com/karmaterminal/openclaw/blob/6db118a2441052e8325b67e2c9b17f7fc6acf419/docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md#51-core-configuration-surface), [§5.3 Cross-session targeting policy](https://github.com/karmaterminal/openclaw/blob/6db118a2441052e8325b67e2c9b17f7fc6acf419/docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md#cross-session-targeting-policy). --- ## Observability — multi-span OTel trace stitching The continuation feature emits OTel spans for the load-bearing lifecycle events. `traceparent` propagates through every continuation hop so reviewers see a single trace tree spanning the full chain rather than orphan runs. Spans emitted (all in `openclaw.*` or `continuation.delegate.dispatch` namespace — no platform/OTel-semconv emissions): | Span | Emitted when | |---|---| | `openclaw.run` | Each agent turn (root + every continuation/delegate-spawned child run) | | `continuation.delegate.dispatch` | Each `continue_delegate` consumption from TaskFlow (one per delegate hop) | | `openclaw.harness.run`, `openclaw.context.assembled`, `openclaw.model.call`, `openclaw.model.usage`, `openclaw.tool.execution` | Existing per-turn observability surfaces, parented under their `openclaw.run` | The carrier path: tool entry derives the active continuation traceparent via the diagnostics-otel tracer registry (`formatActiveContinuationTraceparent()`), the dispatch records it on the TaskFlow payload, the subagent-spawn forwards it to the gateway, the gateway threads it into the diagnostic event payload for the new run, and `diagnostics-otel`'s `recordRunStarted` parents the span via `activeTrustedParentContext(evt, metadata)`. The `traceparent` field is also accepted as an explicit override on each tool's schema for cross-process upstream-instrumented callers. Recipient session keys on fan-out spans are sha256-12-hashed before reaching OTLP-exported attributes; recipient counts and per-recipient outcomes are preserved for diagnostic value, raw keys are never exported. RFC reference: [§6.6–§6.8 Observability](https://github.com/karmaterminal/openclaw/blob/6db118a2441052e8325b67e2c9b17f7fc6acf419/docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md#66-chain-correlation-via-diagnostics-otel). --- ## Real behavior proof **Environment**: OpenClaw gateway `2026.5.12-beta.1` (`6db118a2`) on two independent hosts — urudyne (WSL2, RTX 3080) and DGX Spark (ARM64, Blackwell). `continuation.enabled: true`, `crossSessionTargeting: disabled` (default) + `enabled` (tested). **Behavior tested**: Agent self-elected turn continuation via `continue_work()`, `continue_delegate()`, and `request_compaction()` — including post-compaction lifecycle return, cross-session targeting gate enforcement, and OTel multi-span trace stitching. - **Behavior addressed**: Agent sessions elect their own next turn (`continue_work`), dispatch background sub-agents with typed return modes (`continue_delegate`: normal/silent/silent-wake/post-compaction), and request volitional compaction (`request_compaction`). Chain budget enforcement, cross-session targeting gate, and OTel carrier propagation all operate on real sessions. - **Real environment tested**: OpenClaw gateway `2026.5.12-beta.1` (`6db118a2`) on two independent hosts — Host A: WSL2/x64, RTX 3080 16GB, `github-copilot/claude-opus-4.7`; Host B: ARM64 DGX Spark, 128GB, Blackwell GPU. `continuation.enabled: true`, `crossSessionTargeting: disabled` (default) + `enabled` (tested). - **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: (1) Enable continuation on both hosts via config. (2) Fire `continue_work` from main session — verify wake + chain-counter increment. (3) Fire `continue_delegate` in all four modes (normal, silent, silent-wake, post-compaction). (4) Fire depth-2 chained delegation with `fanoutMode: tree` + cross-session return. (5) Fire `request_compaction` at >70% context — verify threshold gate reject + accept paths. (6) Verify post-compaction delegates fire AT lifecycle event (not on timer). (7) Verify `crossSessionTargeting` gate enforcement at tool-validation, TaskFlow, post-compaction, bracket-syntax. (8) Verify OTel trace stitching across continuation chain (Tempo trace inspection). (9) Verify chat-card rendering of continuation/compaction counters via `/status`. - **Evidence after fix**: Full proof corpus at PR head SHA [`PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/) — see corpus table below. All continuation tool-surfaces (`continue_work` + `continue_delegate` × 4 modes + `request_compaction`) live-fire verified at current PR head, with W3C traceparents stitched server-side under agent-turn span. Earlier corpus iterations preserved at [karmaterminal-openclaw-docs PROOFS chain](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/) for audit. - **Observed result after fix**: All 9 R-* scenarios PASS. Two independent Tempo traces from two independent hosts confirm multi-span OTel stitching. Post-compaction lifecycle coupling proven by event-timing (delegates fire AT compaction, not on timer). `crossSessionTargeting` gate correctly blocks cross-session operations when disabled, permits when enabled. Chat-card `/status` rendering shows continuation and compaction counters. - **What was not tested**: Compaction lifecycle completion on `provider=github-copilot` (known provider-side limitation per issue #625; API surface accepts the request correctly). Cross-session targeting with `fanoutMode: "all"` at fleet scale (tested with 2 hosts). Bracket-syntax `[[CONTINUE_WORK:N]]` fallback (legacy path; tool-call path is primary). ### Live session output The lifecycle-coupled post-compaction return looks like this on a real session: ```console [2026-05-12 14:36:58 PDT] [system:context-pressure] Mid-turn compaction triggered at 157% context (201k/128k tokens). [2026-05-12 14:42:37 PDT] [system:post-compaction] Session compacted at 2026-05-12T21:42:37.655Z. Compaction count: 4. Queued 2 post-compaction delegate(s) for delivery into the fresh session. [2026-05-12 14:42:38 PDT] [continuation:compaction-delegate-spawned] Post-compaction shard dispatched. Mode: post-compaction — fires AT the compaction lifecycle event (not on timer). 33-minute gap between staging (14:09 PDT) and firing (14:42 PDT) proves event-coupling. ``` A `continue_work()` self-elected turn on a main session: ```console Session: agent:main:discord:channel:1466192485440164011 Host: urudyne (Linux 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2, x64, node v25.9.0) Model: github-copilot/claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal Build: OpenClaw 2026.5.12-beta.1 (6db118a) Tool: continue_work Args: {delaySeconds: 10, reason: "R-CW-1 wake-event-evidence capture"} Response: {"status": "scheduled", "delaySeconds": 10, "traceparent": "00-d7a6477c9d91469f5708d23a8d22788f-..."} [continuation:wake] Turn 2/200. Chain started at 2026-05-12T20:56:03.613Z. ``` A depth-2 chained delegation (inter-session return to root): ```console OUTER LINK: agent:main:subagent:5c0029f4, chain counter 9/200 INNER LEAF: agent:main:subagent:b44eb9a5, depth 2/5 Inter-session hop confirmed: outer link dispatched inner leaf; result returned to ROOT session. Runtime: 8s. ``` ## Current PR head + proof corpus **Current head**: [`47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/openclaw/commit/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090) — squashed continuation feature, single commit on `upstream/main@d124c5aa20`. `mergeable=true mergeStateStatus=CLEAN`; 70 SUCCESS / 0 FAILURE / 5 SKIPPING CI rollup. **Substantive proof corpus** at the current PR head: **[`PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/)** — fresh live-fire on the exact byte the PR ships: | Row | Description | Verdict | |-----|-------------|---------| | [`continuation-live-fire.md`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/blob/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/continuation-live-fire.md) | All 4 continuation tool-surfaces fired at current head — `continue_work` + `continue_delegate(silent/silent-wake/post-compaction)` + `request_compaction`; single trace-id `8c27f1a4f8fd5fe7f195490a5f09f2ac` across all delegates; fan-out counter advancing | ✅ PASS | | [`R-TA-1/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/R-TA-1/) | Chain-budget accounting: live `continue_delegate(silent-wake)` fire on deployed gateway; W3C traceparent `00-a92fe1dd0abe8613929d1c625f1c018e-edb0dff2aa71e94e-01`; server-side trace stitched under agent-turn span; 2 cap-enforcement sites byte-walked in deployed `dist/`; full Tempo trace fetch at `http://tempo.dandelion.cult/api/traces/a92fe1dd0abe8613929d1c625f1c018e` (30 spans) | ✅ PASS | | [`R-TA-2/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/R-TA-2/) | Token-counter additivity + post-compaction queue stability across `compactionCount=1` | ✅ PASS | | [`inter-session-targeting/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/inter-session-targeting/) | `continue_delegate` with `targetSessionKey` routing at current head; cross-session fan-out verified | ✅ PASS | | [`post-compaction-threshold/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/post-compaction-threshold/) | Two-tier threshold (config 40% + tool-side 70%); `request_compaction` rejection path; three-layered guards | ✅ PASS | | [`R-TA-1-RECONFIRM/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/R-TA-1-RECONFIRM/) | Runtime-identical-attest reconfirm (response shape byte-identical across the chain) | ✅ PASS | | [`deploy-validation/EVIDENCE.md`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/blob/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/deploy-validation/EVIDENCE.md) | 4-host fleet deploy at current head; cross-validation across 4 distinct hardware substrates; all gateways active | ✅ PASS | | [`README.md`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/blob/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/README.md) + [`METHOD.md`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/blob/main/PROOFS/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/METHOD.md) | Corpus overview + methodology | — | **Substantive feature-proof origin**: [`PROOFS/718d8558eb618304b5cc43c8a3b5d93ff5bef454/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/718d8558eb618304b5cc43c8a3b5d93ff5bef454/) — 8-row continuation-feature proof corpus from earlier squash. Runtime-identical-attest chain across 10 drift-cure hops to current head verified via zero-hunks on 24 load-bearing continuation surface files (Appendix A at [karmaterminal-openclaw-docs#84](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/pull/84)). **Drift-cure history**: this PR underwent multiple drift-cure-rebases as upstream advanced during review; squashed candidate at current head incorporates all upstream evolution. Per-cure audit-trail at [`karmaterminal-openclaw-docs:PROOFS/`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs/tree/main/PROOFS/) chain (cure-(13) → cure-(20)v3). ## RFC Continuation RFC at [`docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md`](https://github.com/karmaterminal/openclaw/blob/47c9280234312e5ed9d9f460d03cac60185d6090/docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md).
